<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The current tests are too minimal. They only cover the health endpoint and registration creation. More meaningful tests are needed.

Planned additions include GUID generation and ID mapping tests. Handler coverage will include empty lists and seeded data. Repository integration tests are also under consideration. The choice is between a new Web test project with mocks or a fake repository in the existing test suite.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Planning additional tests for RegistrationCaseId.New(). One test will verify it returns non-empty GUIDs. Another will verify generated values are unique.

Also planning tests for ToString(), Create(), the alive endpoint, and list endpoint behavior including empty results, seeded data shapes, and descending ID ordering. Considering a Web.Tests project for handler unit tests.

Decided against adding a separate Web.Tests project. Domain tests will cover value objects and entities. Integration tests will cover API endpoints with seeded data. Handler unit tests may be added directly to integration tests or placed in a Web unit test project.

A dedicated `SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web.Tests` project will be created for CurrentOfficer authentication logic. Inline fake repositories in Domain.Tests will handle handler unit tests without full integration.

Scope will stay minimal by adding tests to existing projects rather than creating new ones. Domain.Tests will receive RegistrationCaseIdTests and expanded RegistrationCaseTests. Integration.Tests will gain an alive endpoint test and list endpoint coverage for empty results, seeded data, and descending ID ordering. The list handler unit test will resolve directly from the DI container within Integration.Tests instead of using a separate project.

Adding a Web reference to Domain.Tests would break architectural layering. Handler tests will therefore remain in Integration.Tests, either as full-stack HTTP tests or by resolving the handler directly from the factory’s service provider with seeded data. A helper method on MunicipalAppFactory will be added to simplify seeding registration cases during integration tests.
</details></think>
